label: Planning issue review
description: A scenario focused on the fresh planning issue finder that reviews a completed implementation plan without editing it.

INITIAL_REQUEST_AND_SUBSEQUENT_CONVERSAT.text: Use trycycle to build a static explorer that makes prompt gaps obvious.
The strategy proposes a Python-first static site builder plus Playwright-based integration coverage of the rendered UI.

USER_REQUEST_TRANSCRIPT.text: Use trycycle to build a static explorer that makes prompt gaps obvious.
Keep the sidecar simple and derive as much as possible from SKILL.md and prompt templates.

FULL_CONVERSATION_VERBATIM.text: Use trycycle to build a static explorer that makes prompt gaps obvious.
Testing strategy recommends high-confidence integration coverage of user-visible behavior at each UI step.

bindings.PLANNING_FINDINGS_MEMO:
## Issue 1
Finding: The plan does not state the source of truth for gate ordering.
Evidence:
- The user asked to derive as much as possible from SKILL.md and prompt templates.
- A static sidecar order could drift when SKILL.md changes.

## Issue 2
Finding: The plan does not prove that every planning subprompt is visible in the explorer.
Evidence:
- The planning loop now has separate issue-finder, deepening, synthesis, and nonconvergence prompts.
- A single default prompt tab would hide regressions in the non-default prompt sources.
